description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio, characterized by a rectangular wooden or wood-like case with a dark brown finish. The front panel features a large, cream-colored speaker grille above a black control area, which includes two prominent rotary knobs on either side and a row of push buttons below a frequency display. The frequency display is marked with various bands and stations, typical of mid-20th-century radios. The sides of the radio each have a small, square, cream-colored mesh panel, likely for additional speaker output or ventilation. The back and top are plain, with no visible controls or openings, emphasizing the utilitarian yet classic design.
description: A vintage tabletop radio with a dark brown rectangular case, cream speaker grille, two large knobs, and a row of push buttons on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cream speaker grille, two large rotary knobs, frequency display, and push buttons.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a small, square, cream-colored mesh panel set into the otherwise plain brown side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a featureless, flat brown surface with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the other side, with a small, square, cream-colored mesh panel.
